factors.summary: Input costs are stable or declining due to lower fuel costs and excess carrier capacity.

evidence.text: Excluding the estimated impacts of the decrease in fuel costs, our average truckload rate per mile charged to our customers decreased approximately 5.5 percent in the second quarter of 2020 compared to the second quarter of 2019. Our truckload transportation costs decreased approximately 2 percent, excluding the estimated decrease in fuel costs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Labor costs are decreasing due to short-term cost reductions and reduced headcount.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased primarily due to declines in performance-based compensation and short-term cost reductions, including furloughs and reduced work hours, that were implemented in the second quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to the impact of COVID-19, as indicated by temporary suspension of share repurchases.

evidence.text: The decrease in cash used for share repurchases was due to a decrease in the number of shares repurchased during the six months ended June 30, 2020 as we have temporarily suspended our share repurchase activity near the end of the first quarter of 2020 as we continue to assess the impacts of COVID-19.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Near-term risks include the ongoing impact of COVID-19 on demand and supply chains.

evidence.text: Although there is uncertainty related to the anticipated impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results, we believe that, assuming no change in our current business plan, our available cash, together with expected future cash generated from operations, the amount available under our credit facilities, and credit available in the market, will be sufficient to satisfy our anticipated needs for working capital, capital expenditures, and cash dividends for at least the next 12 months.
evidence.subsection: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intense with margin compression and pricing pressures.

evidence.text: Net revenues. factors.summary: Economic conditions are a significant headwind due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. While the company is cautiously optimistic about its ability to manage through the current challenges, the uncertainty and potential duration of the pandemic pose risks to future financial performance.

evidence.text: The significance of the operational and financial impact to our business will likely depend on how long and widespread this outbreak proves to be. The extent to which COVID-19 impacts our results will depend on future developments, which are highly uncertain and cannot be predicted.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

evidence.text: Although there is uncertainty related to the anticipated impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results, we believe that, assuming no change in our current business plan, our available cash, together with expected future cash generated from operations, the amount available under our credit facilities, and credit available in the market, will be sufficient to satisfy our anticipated needs for working capital, capital expenditures, and cash dividends for at least the next 12 months.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed with declines in truckload and ocean freight due to reduced demand and pandemic effects, but there are signs of growth in intermodal services.

evidence.text: We have already experienced changes in demand, including declines in certain verticals and regions, that exacerbate the conditions we experienced in 2019, along with volatile pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic continued to impact the North American surface transportation market, resulting in significant volatility to freight volumes and carrier costs during the second quarter of 2020. ... The airfreight market experienced significant pricing increases in the second quarter of 2020 resulting from decline in capacity due to a reduction in commercial flights, increased charter flights, and larger than normal shipment sizes due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
evidence.subsection: MARKET TRENDS

evidence.text: Service line volume statistics Truckload (4.5) % 1.5 % LTL (2.0) % 2.5 % Intermodal — % 4.0 %
evidence.subsection: NAST Segment Results of Operations

factors.summary: Demand trends are decelerating due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, with evidence of declines in certain verticals and regions, reduced consumer demand, and significant volatility in pricing and carrier capacity.

evidence.text: We have already experienced changes in demand, including declines in certain verticals and regions, that exacerbate the conditions we experienced in 2019, along with volatile pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic continued to impact the North American surface transportation market, resulting in significant volatility to freight volumes and carrier costs during the second quarter of 2020. ... The impact of reduced consumer demand and production resulted in reduced carrier capacity, most notably in truckload, as many carriers either reduced lanes or exited the market entirely, which caused significant volatility in purchased transportation pricing.
evidence.subsection: MARKET TRENDS

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues decreased driven by the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic which resulted in slowdowns of demand and production in many industry verticals and resulted in lower pricing in truckload and, to a lesser extent, LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: The company is experiencing challenges with pricing power, primarily due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. This includes margin compression from transportation cost volatility and difficulties in raising prices while maintaining customer commitments. Additionally, pricing power has been negatively affected by changes in demand and volatile pricing conditions.

evidence.text: We have already experienced changes in demand, including declines in certain verticals and regions, that exacerbate the conditions we experienced in 2019, along with volatile pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

evidence.text: Despite industry freight volumes declining approximately 21 percent, our NAST truckload and LTL volumes only decreased 4.5 percent and 2.0 percent, respectively, which is reflective of our pricing strategies to ensure we are near the top of our customers' routing guides. We continued to meet our customer commitments despite significant cost of transportation pricing volatility, which resulted in margin compression during the second quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: BUSINESS TRENDS

evidence.text: Net revenues. Our transportation net revenue margin decreased driven by margin compression in truckload services due to the significant transportation cost volatility relative to our contractual customer pricing resulting from the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic as we continued to meet our customer commitments despite moderating declines or, in some instances, increases for the cost of capacity.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: The company acknowledges the presence of geopolitical risks primarily due to the impact of the pandemic on global markets and supply chains. factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with some sectors like retail experiencing elevated demand while others, particularly in industries such as food service, are facing reduced demand and production levels below historical norms due to the pandemic. Additionally, there are mentions of declines in certain verticals and regions, along with volatile pricing, indicating a weakening trend overall.

evidence.text: We have already experienced changes in demand, including declines in certain verticals and regions, that exacerbate the conditions we experienced in 2019, along with volatile pricing.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

evidence.text: Certain industry verticals, such as retail, continued to see the elevated demands similar to those experienced near the end of the first quarter of 2020, while many other industry verticals continued to experience demand and production well below historical levels.
evidence.subsection: MARKET TRENDS

evidence.text: Despite industry freight volume declines, we achieved volume growth in both NAST truckload and LTL services which is reflective of our pricing strategies to ensure we are near the top of our customers' routing guides.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: Organic growth is decelerating, primarily due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on demand and pricing. While the company managed to achieve volume growth in certain services, this was not sufficient to offset the broader decline in revenues and net revenues.

evidence.text: The significance of the operational and financial impact to our business will likely depend on how long and widespread this outbreak proves to be. The extent to which COVID-19 impacts our results will depend on future developments, which are highly uncertain and cannot be predicted.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

evidence.text: Total revenues decreased 7.2 percent to $3.6 billion, driven primarily by lower pricing in truckload and less than truckload (''LTL'') services. Net revenues decreased 11.6 percent to $614.5 million, primarily driven by lower margin in truckload services.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is decreasing due to reduced demand and excess carrier capacity, as well as carriers exiting the market or reducing lanes, leading to increased pricing volatility.

evidence.subsection: MARKET TRENDS

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues decreased driven by the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic which resulted in slowdowns of demand and production in many industry verticals and resulted in lower pricing in truckload and, to a lesser extent, LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: The company is experiencing compression in both gross and operating margins, driven by factors such as declining net revenues, increased cost volatility, transportation cost volatility, and pricing challenges, particularly in truckload services. These challenges have led to a decrease in operating margins and diluted earnings per share.

evidence.text: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to declining net revenues and increased cost volatility.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

evidence.text: Net revenues decreased 11.6 percent to $614.5 million, primarily driven by lower margin in truckload services.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Operating margin of 30.7 percent decreased 200 basis points. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) decreased 13.1 percent to $1.06.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Net revenues. Our transportation net revenue margin decreased driven by margin compression in truckload services due to the significant transportation cost volatility relative to our contractual customer pricing resulting from the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic as we continued to meet our customer commitments despite moderating declines or, in some instances, increases for the cost of capacity.

factors.summary: The company has implemented various cost reduction measures in response to the pandemic, including furloughs, salary cuts, reduced work hours, and the suspension of certain benefits. These actions have contributed to measurable savings, particularly in personnel expenses.

evidence.text: We have taken steps across the organization to reduce costs, including the elimination of all non-essential travel, temporary salary reductions for company executive officers, temporary reductions in cash retainers for board members, temporary suspension of the company match to retirement plans for U.S. and Canadian employees, accelerating the use of paid time off, and furloughing approximately seven percent of our U.S and Canadian employees in the second quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: SIGNIFICANT DEVELOPMENTS

evidence.text: Personnel expenses decreased primarily due to declines in performance-based compensation and short-term cost reductions, including furloughs and reduced work hours, that were implemented in the second quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, particularly due to decreased truckload margins and declines in LTL services. While airfreight margins have increased, this has not been sufficient to offset the negative impact on overall margins from the lower-margin segments.

evidence.text: Transportation net revenue margin decreased due to lower margins in truckload, and to a lesser extent LTL services, partially offset by a significant increase in airfreight margins.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results of Operations—Three Months Ended June 30, 2020 Compared to Three Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Airfreight margins expanded driven by increased pricing as we were able to leverage our contractual airfreight capacity despite significant shortages in the airfreight market.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019

evidence.text: Total transportation revenues decreased driven by the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic which resulted in slowdowns of demand and production in many industry verticals and resulted in lower pricing in truckload and, to a lesser extent, LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Six Months Ended June 30, 2020 compared to Six Months Ended June 30, 2019
